f35b89fb-3cc2-4e1f-8e58-98165c82df08.name: Notes
f35b89fb-3cc2-4e1f-8e58-98165c82df08.content:
Welcome to protoLabs Studio

This is your workspace notepad. Use it to jot down ideas, track decisions, or leave notes for your team.

Quick Start

  • Board — Create features, track progress, and manage your backlog
  • Agents — AI agents pick up features and implement them in isolated branches
  • Auto-Mode — Queue features with dependencies and let agents work autonomously
  • Context Files — Drop coding standards into .automaker/context/ to guide every agent

Useful Commands

If you're using the Claude Code plugin:

  • /board — View and manage the Kanban board
  • /auto-mode start — Start autonomous feature processing
  • /orchestrate — Set up feature dependencies
  • /plan-project — Plan a full project from an idea

Create new tabs to organize your notes by topic.

49ce8e0b-b2d9-43a9-8073-535698843379.name: Infra Update - Staging HTTPS
49ce8e0b-b2d9-43a9-8073-535698843379.content:
Staging Infra Update (2026-03-05)

PWA Install via Tailscale HTTPS

PWA install wasn't showing on staging because browsers require HTTPS. Staging was HTTP-only over Tailscale.

Changes

  • Tailscale HTTPS proxy added to setup-staging.sh — UI on :443, API on :8443. Runs automatically after health check on --start.

  • nginx fix — sw.js and manifest.webmanifest now served with no-cache + Service-Worker-Allowed: / instead of 7-day immutable cache.

  • PWA virtual module stub — Electron dev mode no longer errors on virtual:pwa-register/react.

Access

Use https://ava.<tailnet>.ts.net for PWA-eligible HTTPS on staging.

PRs

  • #1764 — PWA virtual module stub

  • #1765 — Tailscale HTTPS + nginx fix

adca04b8-ba97-466b-862a-0a04a43f5bfe.name: E2E Pipeline Architecture
adca04b8-ba97-466b-862a-0a04a43f5bfe.content:
protoLabs Studio: Idea-to-Production E2E Pipeline

Finalized 2026-03-06. This is the canonical reference for the full autonomous development lifecycle.

Overview

The pipeline has 7 phases. GTM is out of scope (separate initiative). Every phase is event-driven — completion of one phase triggers the next automatically.

SIGNAL INTAKE → IDEATION → PLANNING → EXECUTION → COMPLETION CASCADE → CEREMONIES → PROMOTION

Phase 1: Signal Intake

Service: SignalIntakeService

4 input channels: Discord, GitHub webhooks, MCP tools, UI

Classification: ops (board/agents/infra) | gtm (parked)

Output: Routed signal to appropriate handler

Entry Points

  • Discord suggestion → process_idea → PM Agent triage
  • MCP create_feature → direct to board (backlog)
  • MCP submit_prd → CoS pipeline → PM Agent
  • UI "New Feature" → direct to board (backlog)
  • Bug report → Discord #bug-reports → bug_triage channel workflow

Phase 2: Ideation & Intake

Single Feature Path

PM Agent Pipeline (authority-agents/pm-agent.ts):

triage(opus) → research(sonnet) → PRD(sonnet) → review → approval → feature on board

PM → ProjM handoff is implicit (PM approves, ProjM picks up if project-scale).

Project-Scale Path

Services: ProjectLifecycleService, ProjectOrchestrationService

Idea → Deep Research → SPARC PRD → Review → Approval
  → Milestone decomposition → Phase breakdown
  → ProjectOrchestrationService scaffolds:
    - Epic features (one per milestone)
    - Child features (one per phase)
    - Dependency wiring (topological)
    - Git branches (epic/* and feature/*)

Output: Features on board in BACKLOG with full dependency graph

Phase 3: Planning & Dependency Wiring

Service: DependencyResolverService

  • Topological sort produces execution order
  • Foundation features (isFoundation=true) block downstream until MERGED (not just review)
  • Epic hierarchy: feature/* → epic/* → dev
  • Auto-mode reads execution_order to pick next unblocked feature

Phase 4: Execution (Auto-Mode + Lead Engineer)

Services: AutoModeService, LeadEngineerService, LeadEngineerStateMachine

Auto-Mode Loop

while (backlog has unblocked features) {
  pick next feature by execution_order
  → LeadEngineerService.process(feature)
  wait for completion or failure
  handle result (advance or escalate)
}

LE State Machine

INTAKE → PLAN → EXECUTE → REVIEW → MERGE → DONE
   ↓        ↓       ↓        ↓       ↓
ESCALATE ←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←←┘

Per State

  • INTAKE: Validate feature, check deps satisfied
  • PLAN: Agent generates implementation plan
  • EXECUTE: Agent works in isolated git worktree. Model routing: small→haiku, medium/large→sonnet, architectural→opus. Auto-escalation: 2+ failures → opus.
  • REVIEW: PR created, CI runs, CodeRabbit reviews. PR Maintainer agent handles formatting, thread resolution.
  • MERGE: Auto-merge when all checks pass
  • DONE: Feature complete, triggers completion cascade

14 Fast-Path Rules (pure functions, no LLM)

mergedNotDone, orphanedInProgress, staleDeps, autoModeHealth, staleReview, stuckAgent, capacityRestart, projectCompleting, prApproved, threadsBlocking, remediationStalled, classifiedRecovery, hitlFormResponse, missingCIChecks

Phase 5: Completion Cascade

Service: CompletionDetectorService (event-driven)

feature:done
  → all features in epic done? → epic:done
    → all epics in milestone done? → milestone:completed
      → all milestones in project done? → project:completed

Each level emits events consumed by CeremonySchedulerService.

Phase 6: Ceremonies

Services: CeremonyService, CeremonyStateMachine, CeremonySchedulerService

Ceremony State Machine

awaiting_kickoff → milestone_active → milestone_retro
  → milestone_active (next milestone) | project_retro → project_complete

Ceremony Types

  • Milestone kickoff: Start of work block. Context setting, priority alignment.
  • Milestone retro: LangGraph flow (collect → analyze → report). Reviews what shipped, what broke, lessons learned.
  • Project retro: Final retrospective across all milestones. Archives project.
  • Standup: On-demand status check. Not scheduled on a cadence — triggered by need.

Cadence

milestone kickoff → work (auto-mode) → milestone retro
  → next milestone kickoff → work → milestone retro
  → ... (repeat per milestone)
  → project retro → archive

Phase 7: Promotion Pipeline

Service: StagingPromotionService

dev → staging (Ava-autonomous)

list_staging_candidates
  → readiness check:
    1. CI passing on feature's dev merge commit
    2. No open CodeRabbit threads
    3. Feature marked done on board
    4. No held/rejected flags from previous batch
  → create_promotion_batch
  → cherry-pick onto promotion branch
  → PR into staging (--merge commit, NOT squash)
  → auto-merge

staging → main (HITL-gated)

promote_to_main
  → creates PR + HITL approval form
  → Human approves (form or GitHub)
  → Merge (--merge commit)
  → Tag release
  → Deploy notification to #deployments

Ava NEVER merges staging→main herself.

Observability & Traceability

  • EventLedgerService: Append-only JSONL (events.jsonl). 13 event types: feature.*, agent.*, pr.*, build.*, promotion.*
  • LedgerService: Per-feature-completion metrics (metrics.jsonl). Duration, attempts, model, lines changed.
  • ArchivalService: On project:completed — move to .automaker/archive/, stub at original path.
  • Langfuse: Trace analysis and cost tracking per agent session.
  • TrajectoryStore: Verified execution trajectories for learning flywheel.

Git Flow

feature/* → epic/* (if in epic) → dev → staging → main
                  ↓                       ↓
              (squash OK)          (merge commit ONLY)
  • Feature PRs: target epic branch (if epicId) or dev. Squash allowed.
  • Epic PRs: target dev. Merge commit.
  • dev → staging: Cherry-pick promotion. Merge commit.
  • staging → main: Direct PR. Merge commit. HITL-gated.

Known Gaps (as of 2026-03-06)

  1. Promotion uses --squash (FIXED — now --merge)
  2. No CI enforcement that staging PRs come from dev — could bypass dev QA
  3. Ceremony scheduler fragile on restart — timers lost, state persists but re-hydration is best-effort
  4. CompletionDetector dedup is in-memory — restart could re-fire completion events
  5. Dual ledger system — EventLedger and LedgerService overlap; consider consolidating
